name: HIPAA Security Rule — Administrative, Physical, and Technical Safeguards

controls.title: Security Management Process
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations affecting ePHI — the overarching framework for managing ePHI security.

controls.title: Risk Analysis (Required)
controls.description: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI. The foundation of HIPAA Security compliance and most-cited deficiency by HHS.

controls.title: Risk Management (Required)
controls.description: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level.

controls.title: Sanction Policy (Required)
controls.description: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate.

controls.title: Information System Activity Review (Required)
controls.description: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity — audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

controls.title: Assigned Security Responsibility (Required)
controls.description: Identify the Security Official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by the Security Rule.

controls.title: Workforce Security
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all workforce members have appropriate access to ePHI, and prevent those without access rights from obtaining access.

controls.title: Authorization and/or Supervision (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with ePHI or in locations where it might be accessed.

controls.title: Workforce Clearance Procedure (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to ePHI is appropriate.

controls.title: Termination Procedures (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement procedures for terminating access to ePHI when employment ends or workforce member's access is no longer appropriate.

controls.title: Information Access Management
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI consistent with applicable Privacy Rule requirements.

controls.title: Isolating Healthcare Clearinghouse Function (Required if applicable)
controls.description: If a healthcare clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, implement policies to protect the ePHI from unauthorized access by the larger organization.

controls.title: Access Authorization (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI through access to a workstation, transaction, program, or other mechanism.

controls.title: Access Establishment and Modification (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

controls.title: Security Awareness and Training
controls.description: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of the workforce (including management).

controls.title: Security Reminders (Addressable)
controls.description: Periodic security updates communicated to workforce members.

controls.title: Protection from Malicious Software (Addressable)
controls.description: Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.

controls.title: Log-in Monitoring (Addressable)
controls.description: Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies.

controls.title: Password Management (Addressable)
controls.description: Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords.

controls.title: Security Incident Procedures
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents affecting ePHI.

controls.title: Response and Reporting (Required)
controls.description: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate harmful effects; document incidents and outcomes.

controls.title: Contingency Plan
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence (e.g., fire, vandalism, system failure, natural disaster) affecting systems containing ePHI.

controls.title: Data Backup Plan (Required)
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery Plan (Required)
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of ePHI data.

controls.title: Emergency Mode Operation Plan (Required)
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for protection of ePHI security while operating in emergency mode.

controls.title: Testing and Revision Procedures (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans.

controls.title: Applications and Data Criticality Analysis (Addressable)
controls.description: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components.

controls.title: Evaluation (Required)
controls.description: Perform a periodic technical and non-technical evaluation of compliance with the Security Rule, especially after environmental or operational changes affecting ePHI security.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts (Required)
controls.description: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI on its behalf only with satisfactory assurances that the BA will appropriately safeguard the information — documented in a Business Associate Agreement (BAA).

controls.title: Facility Access Controls
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facility in which they are housed, while ensuring authorized access is allowed.

controls.title: Contingency Operations (Addressable)
controls.description: Establish procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan.

controls.title: Facility Security Plan (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft.

controls.title: Access Control and Validation (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function, including visitor control.

controls.title: Maintenance Records (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement policies to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility related to security (e.g., hardware, walls, doors, locks).

controls.title: Workstation Use (Required)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstations that can access ePHI.

controls.title: Workstation Security (Required)
controls.description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI, to restrict access to authorized users.

controls.title: Device and Media Controls
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

controls.title: Disposal (Required)
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of ePHI, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored — wiping, degaussing, or physical destruction.

controls.title: Media Re-use (Required)
controls.description: Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.

controls.title: Accountability (Addressable)
controls.description: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefor.

controls.title: Data Backup and Storage (Addressable)
controls.description: Create a retrievable, exact copy of ePHI, when needed, before movement of equipment.

controls.title: Access Control
controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights.

controls.title: Unique User Identification (Required)
controls.description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

controls.title: Emergency Access Procedure (Required)
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency.

controls.title: Automatic Logoff (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

controls.title: Encryption and Decryption (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI. Encryption is addressable but is industry standard — AES-256 minimum for ePHI at rest.

controls.title: Audit Controls (Required)
controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.

controls.title: Integrity
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.

controls.title: Mechanism to Authenticate ePHI (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.

controls.title: Person or Entity Authentication (Required)
controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.

controls.title: Transmission Security
controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

controls.title: Integrity Controls (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

controls.title: Encryption in Transit (Addressable)
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt ePHI whenever deemed appropriate. Industry standard: TLS 1.2+ for all ePHI transmissions.

controls.title: Documentation Requirements
controls.description: Maintain written (electronic or paper) policies, procedures, and records required by the Security Rule. Retention: 6 years from creation date OR date last in effect (whichever is later). Reviewed periodically and updated as needed.
